macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P/TSX Surges Nearly 375 Points Amid Canadian Bank Earnings
items.summary: Canada's S&P/TSX composite index climbed 374.63 points to 34,501.96, driven by technology sector strength and solid earnings from major banks like CIBC, RBC, and TD, which reported year-over-year profit increases boosted by trading revenue. U.S. markets were mixed with Dow up slightly at 49,499.20, S&P 500 down 37.27 to 6,908.86, and Nasdaq down 273.70 to 22,878.38.

items.title: Nvidia Earnings Trigger Profit-Taking and Tech Sell-Off
items.summary: Nvidia reported record-beating earnings of $68.13 billion, up 70% year-over-year, sparking initial gains followed by a volatile sell-off in tech and semiconductors, contributing to Nasdaq's decline. Dip-buyers emerged at key moving averages, supporting rebounds amid ongoing profit-taking after 2025's strong gains.

items.title: Semiconductors Slump Post-Nvidia; Nasdaq Targets 24,660 Support
items.summary: Nvidia's strong earnings paradoxically led to a broad sell-off in semiconductors and tech stocks, with Nasdaq eyeing support at 24,660 amid risk-off sentiment and profit-taking. Defensive sectors showed resilience as markets anticipate month-end flows.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $68,000 as Crypto Market Gains $170 Billion
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped over 5% to $68,000 in less than 24 hours, triggering a broad relief rally across major tokens. Ethereum climbed 10%, Solana jumped 8%, and Dogecoin rose 9%, with the total crypto market cap pushing toward $2.5 trillion. Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded $616 million in consecutive inflows led by Fidelity and BlackRock.

items.title: Nearly $9 Billion in Bitcoin and Ethereum Options Expire Today
items.summary: 116,000 BTC options and 206,000 ETH options are set to expire on February 27, representing 20% of total open interest with a nominal value of nearly $9 billion. Bitcoin's maximum pain point is $75,000 with a Put Call Ratio of 0.76, while Ethereum's is $2,200 with a ratio of 0.77.

items.title: OCC Proposes New Stablecoin Framework with 60-Day Comment Period
items.summary: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency has opened a 60-day public comment period for its proposed stablecoin framework, which extends regulatory oversight to foreign issuers seeking US market access. The new regime is expected to take effect by January 2027.

items.title: UK FCA Selects Four Firms for Stablecoin Sandbox Trials
items.summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority has selected four firms, including Revolut, for stablecoin sandbox trials under proposed crypto regulations. The initiative will inform final stablecoin rules expected later in 2026.

items.title: India Launches CBDC Pilot for Food Subsidy Distribution
items.summary: India has launched a pilot program using central bank digital currency for food subsidy distribution in Puducherry, with plans to expand nationwide. The initiative enhances traceability and efficiency in welfare payments.

items.title: Tokenised Gold Protocols Outperform Crypto Market in 2026
items.summary: Tokenised gold protocols have emerged as top performers in 2026, with Tether Gold growing 62% to $3.7 billion and Paxos Gold growing 48% to $2.4 billion since January 1. Meanwhile, the broader crypto market has fallen more than 21% year-to-date.

items.title: Tether and Circle Expand South Korean Operations Amid Regulatory Delays
items.summary: Tether and Circle are expanding operations in South Korea as the country delays regulations for won-backed stablecoins. USDC's market share on Korean exchanges has risen to 10%, reflecting growing stablecoin adoption.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: University of Michigan AI Interprets Brain MRIs in Seconds
items.summary: Researchers at the University of Michigan developed an AI system that analyzes brain MRI scans in seconds with up to 97.5% accuracy, outperforming other tools in identifying neurological conditions and urgent cases. The model was trained on hundreds of thousands of real-world scans and patient histories.

items.title: Dartmouth Celebrates AI Milestone with McGuire Prize to Hany Farid
items.summary: Dartmouth University kicked off its 70th AI anniversary on February 26-27 by awarding the McGuire Family Prize to Hany Farid for innovations in digital forensics, including deepfake detection and PhotoDNA for combating child exploitation imagery. The event highlights AI advancements alongside ethical considerations like bias and hallucinations.

items.title: Chatbots Overemphasize Sociodemographic Stereotypes, Study Finds
items.summary: Researchers report that large language models like GPT-4o and Gemini exaggerate stereotypes based on age, gender, race, and other factors when generating personas, potentially harming representation in high-stakes applications. The study calls for community-centered design guidelines and better auditing to address biases.

items.title: CEOs Rank AI as Top Business Risk Surpassing Geopolitics and Cyber Threats
items.summary: For the first time, CEOs identify AI as the greatest business risk, ahead of geopolitical issues and cyber attacks, according to the Conference Board survey. Despite concerns, CEO confidence is at its highest since Q1 2025, with more planning capital spending boosts.

items.title: Northeastern University and SoftBank Demo Autonomous Agentic AI-RAN at MWC 2026
items.summary: Northeastern University, SoftBank Corp., Keysight, and zTouch Networks are demonstrating LTM-powered autonomous agentic AI-RAN technology at MWC Barcelona 2026, advancing AI integration in telecommunications networks.

items.title: California Pushes AI Safeguards in Schools After Classroom Image Scandal
items.summary: Following an incident with Adobe AI generating inappropriate images in a California elementary school, the state Department of Education released new AI guidelines and plans policy recommendations by July. Districts are shifting from bans to regulated use, with calls for more educator training.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Highguard Launches Raid Rush 5v5 Combat Overhaul
items.summary: Wildlight Entertainment released a massive combat overhaul for Highguard on February 27, pivoting from extraction-based gameplay to pure 5v5 action. The update comes after the game lost 99% of its player base since January, dropping from nearly 100,000 concurrent players to below 600.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Launches with GameSquare Partnership
items.summary: Capcom's Resident Evil Requiem launched on February 27, 2026, with GameSquare expanding its partnership to support the global release through integrated digital, creator, and real-world marketing channels.

items.title: Pokémon Day 2026 Celebrates 30th Anniversary
items.summary: Pokémon Day on February 27 marks the franchise's 30th anniversary with official Pokémon Presents livestream, eBay auctions featuring rare collectibles, and multi-platform celebrations. The Pokémon Company is unveiling new games and updates for fans worldwide.

items.title: Jest Emerges from Stealth with $7M Seed Funding
items.summary: Jest, a messaging app-based gaming marketplace, launched with $7 million in seed funding and a developer-friendly 90/10 revenue split. The platform is currently live in the U.S. and plans to expand to 14 additional countries by Q3 2026.

items.title: Splash Damage Acquires Scum Developer Gamepires
items.summary: UK co-development studio Splash Damage has acquired Gamepires, the Croatian developer behind the open-world survival game Scum, from Jagex for an undisclosed fee.

items.title: New York AG Sues Valve Over Loot Box Gambling Allegations
items.summary: The New York attorney general filed a lawsuit against Valve, alleging that loot boxes in its biggest games constitute illegal gambling.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google Scales Up Advanced Energy Technologies in 2026
items.summary: Google is accelerating deployment of geothermal, nuclear, and long-duration energy storage technologies this year, partnering with companies like Kairos, Fervo Energy, and Energy Dome to help drive down costs across the industry.

items.title: FireFox Gold Presents Exploration Updates at Mining Conference
items.summary: FireFox Gold is presenting at the Red Cloud Pre-PDAC Mining Showcase in Toronto, with CEO and Chairman discussing ongoing exploration programs and the Mustajärvi gold project in Finland.

items.title: Westhaven Gold Presents Strategy Updates at Pre-PDAC Showcase
items.summary: Westhaven Gold's President & CEO Ken Armstrong is presenting at Red Cloud's Pre-PDAC Mining Showcase with updates on company strategy and next-phase initiatives.

items.title: Ethos Participates in Citizens Technology Conference
items.summary: Ethos, a life insurance technology company, is participating in The Citizens Technology Conference in Austin, Texas.
